message:
perf(vdbe): add merge-join scan for materialized IN probes via CountIndexEqRun opcode (bd-wwqen B2)

Replace the SeekGE per-probe-row scan with a sorted merge-join when
the IN subquery was materialized into the autoindex. The previous
approach rewound the index cursor to SeekGE for every probe value;
the new path advances both the autoindex and the target index in
lockstep, comparing keys and advancing whichever is behind.

New CountIndexedInTarget::MaterializedProbeSource variant:
- Distinguishes materialized IN subqueries (which produce a sorted
  autoindex) from direct List IN sets (which may not be sorted)
- extract_count_indexed_in_target() now returns MaterializedProbeSource
  when can_use_once_materialized_in_probe_source() succeeds

count_exists_semijoin_merge_is_safe():
- Validates that the merge-join is safe: the probe source's result
  column must align with the target index's first key column, and
  both must be sorted in the same order

Merge-join codegen (when use_materialized_semijoin_merge is true):
- Rewind both probe and index cursors
- Outer loop: read probe value, enter align_outer label
- Compare index key vs probe value:
  - Lt: advance index cursor (advance_outer), loop back to align_outer
  - Gt: advance probe cursor (next_probe), restart outer loop
  - Eq: emit CountIndexEqRun to count all equal-key rows in the index,
    then advance probe cursor
- IfNullRow on index cursor detects exhaustion → done

Fallback: the original SeekGE-per-probe path is preserved for
non-materialized IN lists.

Test updated: assertion now checks for OpenAutoindex (materialization)
instead of SeekGE in the generated bytecode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
